rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just an 'lgtm' approval), so recall is trivially satisfied by both. Both candidates correctly validate the core rename across all hunks with accurate file/line grounding; X's review is a clean but generic approve-checklist with one unverifiable PR-number citation ('vllm#46022') and only a soft non-actionable note. Y adds two additional grounded, file/line-specific observations directly visible in the diff (the openai_serving_* naming-convention break and the missing chat-completion test coverage), giving it stronger precision and actionability without fabricating anything.
